name: A&V Pawn
description_short: A&V Pawn is a pawn shop in Long Beach specializing in luxury designer goods, sneakers, jewelry, and collectibles. They buy, sell, and loan against authenticated high-end items.
description_long: A&V Pawn operates as a pawn shop in Long Beach, California, positioning itself as a buyer and seller of luxury goods and collectibles. The business model centers on purchasing and reselling authenticated designer items, rare sneakers, fine jewelry, and watches—typical pawn shop functions where customers can either sell items outright or use them as collateral for loans. The company maintains an active e-commerce presence through avpawn.com, where they list current inventory for sale to both local and online customers. Their storefront appears to serve both walk-in customers seeking quick cash against collateral and collectors searching for specific luxury items. A&V Pawn differentiates itself by curating high-end inventory rather than typical pawn shop merchandise. Their inventory includes designer handbags (Gucci, Chanel, Louis Vuitton, Balenciaga), authenticated sneakers (Jordan, Nike, Adidas Yeezy), and fine jewelry including gold and diamond pieces. This specialization in luxury goods suggests they target affluent customers in Long Beach seeking quick liquidity or collectors hunting for specific designer items at negotiated prices. The website provides no information about loan terms, interest rates, loan duration, fees, or lending policies. No details appear regarding authentication processes, appraisal methods, or return policies. The site lacks transparency on operational hours, physical location specifics, or how to initiate a pawn transaction. This absence of critical consumer information is typical but notable for a finance-adjacent business.
address: 3323 E South St, Long Beach, CA 90805

company_info.headquarters: 3323 E South St, Long Beach, CA 90805

pros: Specializes in authenticated luxury goods (Gucci, Chanel, Louis Vuitton, Balenciaga) rather than general merchandise
Maintains active online inventory available for nationwide purchase through e-commerce platform
Focuses on high-demand collectible sneakers including limited Jordan and Yeezy releases
Handles fine jewelry and precious metals (10K gold, diamonds) with apparent expertise
Operates in Long Beach, a major metropolitan area with strong demand for luxury goods
Provides immediate cash transactions for customers needing liquidity against collateral
cons: Website provides zero transparency on loan terms, APR, fees, or repayment periods
No information disclosed about authentication credentials, appraisal methodology, or quality guarantees
Missing critical details about physical location, hours of operation, and how to conduct transactions
No customer reviews, ratings, or third-party verification visible on website
Pawn loans typically involve high interest rates and short repayment periods that can trap borrowers in debt cycles
best_for: Collectors and enthusiasts seeking specific luxury sneakers or designer items at negotiated prices
Individuals with high-end goods needing short-term collateral-based loans for immediate cash
Sellers of authenticated designer items or luxury goods looking for quick payment without consignment delays
diagnosis: A&V Pawn is best for affluent consumers or collectors in Long Beach needing quick cash against luxury collateral, or those seeking specific designer/sneaker items at pawn shop prices. The major caveat is the complete lack of transparency regarding loan terms, rates, and policies—customers must visit in person or call to understand actual costs, and pawn loans universally carry high interest rates and short terms that can lead to financial difficulty if items aren't reclaimed.
services: Pawn loans against luxury goods and jewelry collateral
Purchase and sale of authenticated designer handbags
Limited edition and collectible sneaker sales
Fine jewelry and precious metal appraisal and purchasing
Online retail sales through e-commerce platform
Designer shoe and footwear specialization (Nike, Jordan, Adidas, Balenciaga)
